title: The Last Doctor Lives Above
description: On pages 14–16, the last doctor lives above as dalton shelters the crew and explains Drum Island’s fall under Wapol while the search for the island’s last doctor continues.
scene: The Last Doctor Lives Above. Dalton shelters the crew and explains Drum Island’s fall under Wapol while the search for the island’s last doctor continues. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Are you crazy!? Just what are you trying to put Nami through!?

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: It's fine, I'll be carrying her.

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Even so, you're still going to exacerbate her condition!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: What's the big deal. We gotta get her to a doctor as quickly as possible, don't we?

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Yes but it's impossible as it is right now! Just look how tall that mountain is!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: I can climb it.

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Maybe YOU can, but it's still too much of a burden on Nami!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Well... even if she falls, there's only nice and comfy snow below...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: A normal person would die instantly if they fell from a mountain of that height!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: And you're not even dealing with a normal, healthy person here! She has a temperature 6 degrees above normal!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Do you under- stand how serious her situation is!?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: ...haha.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: NAM...!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Nami!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I have to... get well quick...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: ...I'll leave it to you then, Luffy.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Great!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Just leave it to me!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Oh Lord, our Navigator's just as crazy as our captain!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Do you understand just how sick you are, NAM!!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Mister, gimme some meat!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Nami, are you really fine with this!? It may take over several hours to climb up.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Meat...?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Alright,

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: I'll be coming along too!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: letting Nami fall even ONCE could mean the end for her! got it!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What!? Even once!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Hold on... stand still! I have to tie her tightly around you...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: tighten!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: That should do.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: I'll stay behind here. I'd only drag you down if I came along.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Me too!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Alright! Hang on tight, Nami!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: OK...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: If you're serious about this, I won't stop you but you should at least climb up from the other side...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: The route to the top from here is full of lapins...! They're extremely violent and carnivorous rabbits... If you come across a pack of them, there'll be no escaping alive!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Rabbits? But we're in a hurry... I'm sure it'll be fine.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Right?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: I'll kick any that get in our way.

summary: This part centers on the last doctor lives above, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
